description: Canonical test vectors for the SEP Anarchy Soroban contract (`contracts/sep-anarchy`). PLONK-migration era: VKs are no longer constructor inputs or rotatable storage; they are baked into the contract WASM via `include_bytes!` from `contracts/plonk-verifier/tests/fixtures/`. Anarchy is the simplest of the per-type contracts: 2-PI membership flow, 3-PI update flow, no occupancy commitment, no quorum threshold. Member count is informational only.
architecture_note: This contract is the per-type Anarchy contract — one of the eventual four (Anarchy, OneOnOne, Democracy, Oligarchy). The `group_type` discriminator is implicit in the contract address; there is no polymorphic `update_commitment` dispatch, no `group_type` field in storage, no type-confusion guard. Anarchy is the protocol's null case: no quorum threshold, no admin set, no occupancy commitment for member-count hiding.

verification_backend.description: All proof verification routes through the shared `plonk-verifier` crate (`plonk_verifier::verifier::verify`). VKs are baked: no admin rotation, no per-tier storage. Rotating a VK requires a contract redeploy.
verification_backend.proof_format: 1601-byte arkworks-uncompressed serialised TurboPlonk proof (BLS12-381). Wire type: BytesN<1601>.
verification_backend.vk_format: 3002-byte arkworks-uncompressed serialised jf-plonk VerifyingKey (BLS12-381). Embedded via include_bytes! from contracts/plonk-verifier/tests/fixtures/{vk,update-vk}-d{5,8,11}.bin.
verification_backend.srs_g2: Single 96-byte compressed [τ]_2 from the EF KZG ceremony, shared across all six baked VKs. Embedded from contracts/plonk-verifier/tests/fixtures/srs-g2-compressed.bin.
verification_backend.fixture_drift_anchor: contracts/plonk-verifier/tests/fixtures/* are produced by the off-chain `circuit::plonk::verifier::tests::plonk_verifier_fixtures_match_or_regenerate` test. Run without STELLAR_REGEN_FIXTURES=1 to verify the on-chain bytes still match what the prover side would emit today.

error_codes.description: Numeric values of `Error` variants (`#[contracterror] #[repr(u32)]`). PLONK-migration drops `InvalidVkLength=9` and `InvalidPoint=26` — both were Groth16-only paths (constructor IC validation and subgroup checks against `VerificationKeyData`). The active set is 15 reachable variants.

vectors.name: Reserved3
vectors.note: previously Unauthorized; admin checks now use require_auth() which panics

vectors.name: AdminOnly
vectors.note: create_group in restricted mode rejects non-admin callers (caller != admin after caller.require_auth)

dropped_in_plonk_migration.name: InvalidVkLength
dropped_in_plonk_migration.reason: VKs are baked, not user-supplied; no IC-count check possible (or relevant — PLONK VKs have a different shape)

dropped_in_plonk_migration.name: InvalidPoint
dropped_in_plonk_migration.reason: PLONK verifier traps inside Soroban host primitives (g1_msm / pairing_check) on off-curve inputs rather than returning Result::Err; subgroup-validation as a contract-level error is no longer applicable

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: UnknownVkKind
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: VkKind exhaustively matched in update_vk; the variant was unreachable

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: MemberCountMismatch
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: Democracy v1 artifact; Anarchy member_count is informational and never enforced by the contract

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: MemberCountOutOfRange
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: Democracy v1 artifact; same as above

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: GroupTypeMismatch
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: no polymorphic dispatch in single-type contract

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: InvalidInitialMembership
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: no in-circuit floor for Anarchy; the natural 'no witness, no proof' game-theoretic floor is sufficient

tier.description: Member tier index → tree depth → leaf capacity. The contract MUST reject `tier > 2` with `InvalidTier`. Tier capacity is the total slot space (cumulative-joins ceiling per Democracy §4.1's tombstone-permanence rule, inherited).

max_groups_per_tier.description: Cross-group cap enforced by create_group: per-tier active-group count must be < MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER, else TierGroupLimitReached.
max_groups_per_tier.rule: GroupCount(tier) MUST be < 10000 at create_group; exceeded → TierGroupLimitReached

create_group_validation.description: Per-call validation in `create_group`. Single-type contract — no per-type dispatch. No threshold (Anarchy has no quorum); no occupancy_commitment (Anarchy doesn't hide counts).
rules: commitment MUST be a canonical Fr encoding (Fr::from_bytes ∘ Fr::to_bytes round-trip); non-canonical → InvalidCommitmentEncoding
public_inputs MUST be exactly 2 elements `[commitment, be32(0)]` matching the wire `commitment` arg and the create-time epoch=0 invariant; mismatch → PublicInputsMismatch
group_id MUST NOT already be assigned to a live or inactive group; collision → GroupAlreadyExists
GroupCount(tier) MUST be < MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER (10000); exceeded → TierGroupLimitReached
if RestrictedMode is true: caller MUST equal Admin, else AdminOnly (caller.require_auth() runs first)
proof_hash(proof) MUST NOT be in UsedProof storage; collision → ProofReplay
proof MUST verify under the baked membership VK at `tier` against `public_inputs`; failure → InvalidProof

member_count_validation.description: member_count is informational only — the contract accepts any u32 value and stores it without validation. The v1 sentinel `0` means 'not tracked'. The contract NEVER updates this field after create.

update_commitment_wire_format.description: Wire format for `update_commitment`. Caller supplies `proof: BytesN<1601>` and `public_inputs: Vec<BytesN<32>>`. The contract reads `c_old / epoch_old` from storage and validates `public_inputs[0..2]` against them; `public_inputs[2]` (c_new) is the next commitment. No separate `c_new` wire arg.

wire_fields.name: proof
wire_fields.rule: TurboPlonk proof bytes; failure-modes go via plonk-verifier::ParseError or PairingMismatch, both surfaced as InvalidProof

public_input_validation: public_inputs[2] is canonical Fr, else InvalidCommitmentEncoding
update_commitment_wire_format.post_state: On success, the group's `commitment` advances to public_inputs[2], `epoch` increments by 1, the previous entry is appended to History (FIFO-pruned at HISTORY_WINDOW=64), and the proof's SHA-256 is recorded in UsedProof.

verify_membership_wire_format.description: Read-only wire format for `verify_membership`. No nullifier consumption; same proof bytes can be re-submitted indefinitely.
verify_membership_wire_format.active_check: verify_membership intentionally does NOT check state.active — read-only against any historical state. Returns Ok(false) on bad proof rather than InvalidProof.

storage_layout.description: Storage entries written by the contract. Per-type Anarchy means `group_type` is implicit in the contract address (not a stored field). `member_count` is preserved as informational (per option-A choice in the design doc).

fields.name: epoch
fields.rule: starts at 0, increments by 1 per successful update_commitment

fields.name: timestamp
fields.rule: ledger timestamp when this state was recorded

fields.name: active
fields.rule: always true in current contract — `deactivate_group` was removed in postmortem #153, so no production code path sets this to false. Field + GroupInactive guard retained as defense-in-depth for any future re-introduction of deactivation.

fields.name: member_count
fields.rule: informational only; supplied at create_group; NEVER updated by the contract. Sentinel value 0 means 'not tracked' per v1 sep-xxxx convention. The contract is value-agnostic to this field.

removed_vs_sep_xxxx.rationale: single-type contract; the contract address IS the discriminator

data_keys.name: RestrictedMode
data_keys.note: when true, only the admin may call create_group; toggled by set_restricted_mode (admin-only). Default false.

data_keys.name: History(group_id)
data_keys.note: rolling-window history of past entries; capped at HISTORY_WINDOW=64; pruned FIFO

data_keys.name: GroupCount(tier)
data_keys.note: active group count per tier; checked against MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER (10000) in create_group. Monotonic increment-only since `deactivate_group` was removed (postmortem #153).

removed_in_plonk_migration.rationale: membership VKs are baked into the WASM via include_bytes!; rotation requires redeploy
update VKs are baked into the WASM via include_bytes!; rotation requires redeploy

admin_entrypoints.description: Admin-gated entrypoints. All require admin.require_auth(); panic on unauth. There is no separate `set_admin` entrypoint — admin is fixed at __constructor.

vectors.name: set_restricted_mode
vectors.purpose: when restricted=true, create_group rejects non-admin callers with AdminOnly. Storage: DataKey::RestrictedMode (instance). Default value (key absent) is treated as false. Emits RestrictedModeChanged event for audit transparency.

removed_in_plonk_migration.name: update_vk
removed_in_plonk_migration.rationale: VKs are baked; rotation requires redeploy

queries.description: Read-only entrypoints. None require auth. All return Result<_, Error>.

args.name: max_entries
args.rule: tail-truncated to most-recent `max_entries`; if max_entries ≥ history.len() returns all entries

vectors.name: verify_membership
vectors.note: returns Ok(false) on bad proof rather than InvalidProof — read-only verifier semantics. Does NOT check state.active.

vectors.name: bump_group_ttl
vectors.note: permissionless TTL bump for long-lived groups; bumps Group + History TTLs only, NOT UsedProof

epoch_invariant.description: Anarchy keeps Democracy's monotonic-by-1 epoch rule. update_commitment increments stored.epoch by exactly 1 on success.

proof_replay_protection.description: Global nullifier scope. The nullifier is sha256(proof_bytes) over the 1601-byte proof. Scope is global per contract: the same 1601 bytes used at any state-changing entrypoint on any group consume the nullifier.
rules: Once a proof's bytes have been recorded by record_proof, any subsequent entrypoint call with byte-identical proof is rejected with ProofReplay.
TTL of UsedProof entries is LEDGER_BUMP (~30 days). After expiry the same proof bytes can be re-used; long-lived groups require periodic state advancement to keep nullifiers alive.
Pre-inclusion mempool replay is NOT closed by the contract-level nullifier — circuit-level operation tag binding would close that (deferred per design §11).

tests_to_implement.description: Inline test names that the `#[cfg(test)] mod test` block in `contracts/sep-anarchy/src/test.rs` MUST cover. The `test_vectors_consistency` test loads this very file and asserts the contract's `Error` enum / tier mapping / MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER constant match the values pinned above.
